---
title: " "
pagetitle: "Lonnie Ernst | About"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    df_print: paged
    css: styles.css
---

<div class="page-title">About</div>

<div class="about-row">
<div class="about-photo circle">
<img src="images/headshot.jpg" alt="Lonnie Ernst">
</div>
<div class="about-text">

My path into biotechnology started with a biology course where we covered the concepts of climate change. I was fascinated by how much we're able to understand about the systems that sustain life, and drawn in by the possibility of using that knowledge to create a healthier planet. This course led me to take a biotechnology elective, which gave me hands-on experience working in a lab and allowed me to see biotech in action in real time. This grew into a B.S. in Biotechnology and a career spanning both the research bench and GMP manufacturing.

Over the past several years, I've built experience across the full range of laboratory work: from molecular biology and microbiology in academic and research settings to reagent formulation in a regulated GMP production environment. Working in both worlds has given me a perspective I value: I understand not just how to develop and troubleshoot a procedure at the bench, but what it takes to run it reliably, at scale, under rigorous quality standards.

</div>
</div>

What draws me most is the intersection of molecular biology and real-world application. My current research explores engineering cyanobacteria to break down persistent organic pollutants while suppressing toxin production. It's the kind of problem where careful bench work meets a tangible environmental goal. This project has been an exciting experience for me, as it has challenged my wet lab, research, and computational abilities. I'm driven by the positive impact that biotechnology can have on environmental and human health.

Increasingly, I'm also drawn to bringing data analysis and computational tools into the lab, translating raw data into insights people can act on. The implementation of computational tools to better process, store, and manage data plays a key role in better understanding the world around us.

<div class="about-row reverse">
<div class="about-text">

Outside of the lab, I'm often found tending my garden, exploring new PC and other tech hardware, or playing Slay the Spire. I enjoy working on projects that challenge the way I think, explore new methods to optimize or improve what I have, encourage self-sufficiency, and give back to the environment and people around me.

</div>
<div class="about-photo landscape">
<img src="images/personal.jpg" alt="Outside of work">
</div>
</div>
